coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 8–10 miles, Z2 only (130–135 bpm), no vert. Your resting HR is 8 bpm above your 30-day average and recovery score is 54/100—that's moderate fatigue creeping in with 77 days to the race. This is taper week 1; you need to shed volume smartly while staying sharp. A shakeout run tomorrow clears the legs before your long run Saturday. Keep effort genuinely easy—no temptation to 'build fitness' in week 23.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 8.89 hours last night (strong), so fuel aggressively during the run: 30–40g carbs per hour (gel, sports drink, or real food if you can stomach it). Your last two runs averaged 139–144 bpm; today's Z2 effort will be lower intensity but you'll run ~90 min, so don't under-fuel. Post-run: 20–30g protein + 50–80g carbs within 30 min to arrest the elevated resting HR and prime recovery before Saturday's big vert day.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Sleep quality last night was excellent (81.6% efficiency), but your 30-day average sits at only 7.07 hours—taper is the time to bank sleep, not short-change it. Aim for 8.5+ hours tonight; your elevated resting HR suggests accumulated stress. Add 10–15 min of easy foam roll and dynamic stretching post-run. Skip any ice baths; you're not in peak training anymore, and cold immersion blunts adaptation signals during taper.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're at week 23 of a 22-week plan, which means you've done the hard work. The Julian Alps 50K is 77 days out—enough time to sharpen but not enough to rebuild if you break down now. Taper brain is real; the urge to 'make up' volume or push hard is a trap. Trust the training log: 48.8 miles last week into 37.1 this week is the right pattern. Use Friday's easy run to mentally rehearse a leg of the climb and visualize pacing discipline at altitude.
